task_completion.description: The Emoncms Graph page is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser displays the Emoncms interface with the Graph module active (URL should show http://localhost/graph or similar).

task_completion.description: The 'Solar PV' feed is selected for graphing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the feed selection menu, list, or graph legend, 'Solar PV' is checked, highlighted, or explicitly listed as an active data source for the graph.

task_completion.description: The graph successfully renders the data for the Solar PV feed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A chart (e.g., line or area graph) is visible in the main display area showing plotted data. The y-axis should indicate values in Watts (W) with peaks around 2500W, and the visual pattern should show daily peaks (midday) and valleys (zero at night).

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser navigating the Emoncms web interface, rather than terminal commands, API calls, or scripts.

integrity.description: The graph is genuinely rendered by the Emoncms application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph appears as an interactive web element typical of the Emoncms Graph module, not a static image opened in an image viewer or a manually drawn chart.

integrity.description: The page content was not altered using developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Feed names and graph labels appear with consistent fonts, alignment, and styling matching the rest of the Emoncms interface, with no developer console open modifying the DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The application is Emoncms. The Graph page is located at http://localhost/graph. The target feed is named 'Solar PV' (unit: W, tag: solar). The data is synthetic historical data covering the last 30 days. The graph should display a realistic solar generation pattern: peaks around 2550W during midday and 0W during night hours.
